task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into OpenClinica and set the active study to Phase II Diabetes Trial (DM-TRIAL-2024).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenClinica interface with the user logged in (e.g., 'root' in the top right) and the active study listed as 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' or 'DM-TRIAL-2024' in the study selection dropdown or header.

task_completion.description: Verify the gender for subject DM-101 was updated to Male.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Subject Matrix, View Subject page, or a success confirmation message showing subject DM-101 with Gender set to 'Male' or 'M'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Date of Birth for subject DM-102 was updated to June 22, 1958.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Subject Matrix, View Subject page, or a success confirmation message showing subject DM-102 with Date of Birth set to '1958-06-22' or '22-Jun-1958'.

task_completion.description: Verify the gender for subject DM-103 was updated to Female.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Subject Matrix, View Subject page, or a success confirmation message showing subject DM-103 with Gender set to 'Female' or 'F'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Date of Birth for subject DM-103 was updated to December 3, 1975.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Subject Matrix, View Subject page, or a success confirmation message showing subject DM-103 with Date of Birth set to '1975-12-03' or '03-Dec-1975'.

task_completion.description: Verify that no other subjects or fields were modified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Review the Subject Matrix or recent activity logs in the screenshots to ensure only DM-101, DM-102, and DM-103 show recent edits, and no other demographic fields (like enrollment dates) were altered for these three subjects.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenClinica web graphical user interface to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser interacting with the OpenClinica web application forms, rather than terminal windows executing SQL commands (e.g., psql) or curl requests.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenClinica application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should display standard OpenClinica branding, navigation menus, and form layouts, not a generic text editor or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a python or bash script to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should not show the agent writing or executing scripts in a terminal to interact with the database directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Study Identifier: DM-TRIAL-2024. Expected corrections: DM-101 (Gender: Male/m), DM-102 (DOB: 1958-06-22), DM-103 (Gender: Female/f, DOB: 1975-12-03). Verify these exact values are reflected in the OpenClinica web interface.
